posture: third-HARD-6-fire (matrix-filling: §VII catalog entry #58; testing TensorAlgebra-subsystem convergence beyond §I+§X)

encoding_attempts.attempt: EQUIV(symbol_T, symbol_T', equivalence_class_id='GL_n_x_GL_n_x_GL_n_action_iso', witness_type=??, witness=...). EQUIV's three witness types are proof_ref / finite_check / equiv_chain. None of them carries a group-action witness (an explicit triple (A, B, C) in GL_n cubed).
encoding_attempts.blocker: EQUIV's witness types don't include 'group_action_witness'. finite_check could fit small-format tensors (2x2x2) where exhaustive search over invertible matrices is feasible — but returns boolean, not the witnessing (A,B,C). proof_ref could point to an external proof but loses substrate-grade verifiability. equiv_chain doesn't apply to non-syntactic equivalence.
encoding_attempts.verdict: PARTIAL — EQUIV exists but witness types incomplete

encoding_attempts.attempt: OperatorPortabilityCertificate(operator='GL_n_action', source_chart='tensor_iso:T', target_chart='tensor_iso:T_prime', transfer_method=STRUCTURAL_ANALOGY, ...). The operator 'GL_n action' applies (A, B, C) to T to get T'.
encoding_attempts.blocker: T030 has the right shape (operator transports T to T') but doesn't carry the GROUP STRUCTURE — substrate has no GroupAction primitive that knows about group composition, inverses, identity. The witness for tensor isomorphism MUST come from a group; OperatorPortabilityCertificate treats the operator as a black box. Loses isomorphism-class semantics.

encoding_attempts.attempt: MethodSpec(engine='magma', strategy='tensor_isomorphism', independence_class=??, version='2.27'). Wrap a tensor-isomorphism test as a MethodSpec; the test's output (true/false + optional witness) is then a CLAIM.
encoding_attempts.blocker: Same enum-gap as fire #39: no IndependenceClass value for GROUP_ACTION_INVARIANT. Even with one added: MethodSpec operates at the call-engine level, not the ALGEBRAIC-STRUCTURE level. Substrate could RUN a Magma iso-test but couldn't represent the equivalence-class structure (orbit, stabilizer, fundamental domain) as substrate-grade data.

Works for the NEGATIVE direction (T ≢ T'): the exclusion certificate carries a Galois-orbit-invariant or other non-iso obstruction. BUT the substrate has no companion POSITIVE primitive — the (A,B,C) witness for T ≅ T' has no substrate-grade home. Same asymmetric-existential pattern as fire #39's ContractionOrderWitness. Strongly suggests this asymmetry is substrate-WIDE, not tensor-specific.
encoding_attempts.verdict: PARTIAL — non-iso encodable, iso witness has no home

capability_gaps_identified.needed_for: completing the existential symmetry — ANY 'is-isomorphic-to' / 'is-equivalent-to' decision needs a constructive POSITIVE primitive
capability_gaps_identified.broader_pattern: Same ASYMMETRIC EXISTENTIAL gap as fire #39's ContractionOrderWitness. Two fires from different paradigms (P30 + GI-hard) hit the same shape — strongly suggests substrate-wide design issue, not tensor-specific.
capability_gaps_identified.convergence_with_fire_39: Fire #39 surfaced ContractionOrderWitness for combinatorial optimization upper bound; fire #40 surfaces IsomorphismCertificate for equivalence-decision upper bound. Both are positive-existentials-with-witness; both lack a substrate-grade home. The unifying primitive is ConstructiveExistenceWitness with role-specific subclasses.

lane_12.convergence_status:
Three fires (#38/#39/#40), three sections (§I/§X/§VII), three paradigms (P28-P31 + P30 + P30/P31). Convergence increasingly clear:
  - TensorObject + TensorNetworkGraph + GroupAction = tensor-specific subsystem (foundational primitives)
  - RankDecompositionWitness + ContractionOrderWitness + IsomorphismCertificate = SAME asymmetric-existential pattern (positive-existential-with-witness substrate-wide gap)
  - MomentPolytope + RewriteSearchTree + OrbitStratification = optimization-geometry-over-discrete-structure family
Pattern: foundational tensor primitives + asymmetric-existential general primitive + optimization-geometry family. Three subsystems, not one — but they interlock.
